Mod 3- Workbook Walkthrough (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 3
Speaker 1: Hey, guys, and welcome to the workbook for module 3. And what I really want to do
here is I just want to quickly recap some of the main takeaways from the previous lesson
to just make sure they're fresh and present before we get into the work. And then we're
going to explore the questions, the direct response 2.0 styled questions pulled from the
47 questions workbook that are really going to help fuel the messaging and extract the
content needed to work on the theme plates and then we're going to take a look at
theme plates themselves and separate lessons right underneath this one to go deeper
into each one and giving you the tools and the encouragement to execute them.
With that in mind, segmentation. So segmentation can happen automatically. Someone
opts in for lead magnet or signs up to your list via Facebook ad or partner traffic. But it
can also come in the form of a call out email that gets your prospect on that special
launch list. And the big point here is that even if you plan to market your offer to your
entire list, it's still a good idea to send out that call out email, which would give your
prospect a chance to just feel excited about the outcome. But more importantly, here's
the big one, just feel exclusively empowered to achieve it and commit towards achieving
it in the most friction free part of your funnel. This is where we open up that ever
powerful consistency loop where we get to say, "I thought you wanted this. You told me
you wanted this. And now that there's an offer, you're backtracking." So that's really
what you want to hold them accountable to. Not in a manipulative, sleazy way but in a
way that like "You really wanted this. Here is your chance." It's not that there's a
product or an investment in front of you, there's an opportunity to do it. So that's what
we're aiming for. That's why you want to make sure you are sending out that call out
email wherever possible.
And then in the confirmation sequence, we learned that this is the most under
leveraged email in most marketing funnels because not only is this usually the most
open email in the sequence, but it's your first real chance to diagnose and reflect back
intent while adding extra layers of meaning to the previous action. This is where it's no
longer just a benign action like you got your newsletter, your lead magnet, or video
sequence. Woo-hoo, right? This is where you could actually reflect back motivation and
diagnose it which builds that trust, builds that credibility and that magical sense of,
"Wow. This person really gets me." And that's what you're going for because that's what
will set the stage for the conversion later on. And while this can arguably be
accomplished as a video on the landing page or the confirmation page, I highly
recommend using it as email primarily first and foremost. It's just kind of an easier way
to grasp it in my opinion. But there's no harm in doing both.
So confirmation email, super under leveraged. This is your chance to really do it the
right way and use it as a tool for the final conversion, not just as a benign thing
confirming a benign action. So what we're coaching, what these two subsequences
combine are coaching and self-selection. It's excitement around the outcome. It's
exclusively empowered to achieve it based on those achievements, circumstances, or
other preconditions that put them at a unique advantage to gain the outcome and allow
them to cash in on those privilege points because that feels good. There's that loss
aversion and not cashing in on privilege points. So give your prospect that chance. And
the opportunity to really get that buy in in the most friction free environment. There's
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Mod 3- Workbook Walkthrough (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 2 of 3
no offer here, there's no harm in just saying, "Yeah, I want that," because there's no
offer to get in the way of it or to really create that friction. So open that consistency
loop that will close in the sales sequence.
Then we're selling the meaning behind the clicks, so the intent, the motivation, what
they'll no longer stand for or accept, what they're ready to do. What are they taking a
stand for by making that click? So add meaning to it. Don't make it that benign, technical
action of just clicking a mouse and opting in. Give it some more power and juice behind
it.
And then I just wanted to explore a few different sources of exclusive empowerment.
We already did this before. We have this over here. So we can be looking at previous
distinctions and accomplishments so author of a best selling book, speaks basic Italian,
already has a six figure business, and so forth. So that could be one source. It could also
be situational, circumstantial, or geographical. They could be a father of a newborn
child, working remotely, recently laid off, all these things could work and it could also be
things they've endured because remember, this is what we feel a lot of pride around.
We really take a lot of pride in our battle scars. So let those battle scars be worth
something. Let those battle scars be the entry ticket into a new, awesome, amazing
opportunity.
So here's the big part. These are the direct response 2.0 questions to explore and it's
possible you've already seen them. If you did the 47 questions all in one shot, but if not
here they are again. So what skills, characteristics, experiences, circumstances, or
achievements or points of pride exclusively empower your prospect to achieve the end
result and/or embark on the journey? So is it that their website already has 2,000
visitors per month? Is it that they already speak basic French? Is it that they only just
began losing their hair? Whatever that source of exclusive empowerment is, write it
down here. And what missteps, regrets, sunk costs, or other sources of disillusionment
might they need to forgive themselves or they ask for forgiveness for, which you'll see in
the [inaudible 00:06:17] template below the Bieber template. Before being able to get
excited about the outcome again. So this is extremely powerful. Sometimes we just
need somebody to say, "Sorry. On behalf of all those things that have just steered us
wrong," before we feel excited and courageous enough to reembark.
And how can any of those previous failures be reframed as necessary prerequisites for
where they are now? So this ties in with that exclusive empowerment, but it gives them
a chance to make that pain worth something, to cash in on that, to cash in on a previous
failure. Cashing in on a previous failure to embark on a new opportunity makes that
previous failure kind of feel good now. It's been kind of forgiven on a smaller level. Not
as big as the one you're going to explore later in the closing sequence with your product
now involved. But how can those previous failures, what struggles can they bring to the
table and cash in as an entry ticket to this journey?
And here are just some additional questions. These aren't in the 47 questions book, but
these are ones I want you to explore right now. In what ways can you add meaning to
the click? So what does it say about them, their commitment level, what they really
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
